exploration_log.aha_moment: WrapResponseWriter.Status() returns 0 when no header has been written. Wrapping the response writer before passing to the handler lets us check whether the handler already started a response.
exploration_log.hindsight: No dead ends; the task description was explicit about using WrapResponseWriter and checking Status() == 0.

key_decisions.decision: Use WrapResponseWriter as specified in the task description
key_decisions.reasoning: Already provided in the middleware package; Status() == 0 is the correct sentinel for 'no response started'.

search_sequence.reasoning: Task description pointed directly to this file.
search_sequence.result: Confirmed: w.WriteHeader(504) is called unconditionally after DeadlineExceeded, ignoring whether the handler already wrote headers.
Status() returns 0 when no header has been written, confirming Status() == 0 is the right check.

solve_notes: Wrapped the response writer with `NewWrapResponseWriter` before passing it to the handler. In the deferred cleanup, changed the unconditional `w.WriteHeader(504)` to check `ww.Status() == 0` first — only writing the 504 if no response has already been started. The handler now also receives `ww` instead of `w`.
task_text:
Fix Timeout middleware silently dropping 504 when response is already started

The `Timeout` middleware in `middleware/timeout.go` writes
`w.WriteHeader(http.StatusGatewayTimeout)` in its deferred cleanup
function after `next.ServeHTTP(w, r)` returns. If the handler detected
the expiring context and called `w.WriteHeader` or `w.Write` before
returning, Go's `http.ResponseWriter` ignores the subsequent
`WriteHeader(504)` call because headers are already committed. The
client receives whatever partial response the handler started, with no
504 status. Fix the middleware to wrap `w` with `WrapResponseWriter`
from `middleware/wrap_writer.go` before passing it to the handler, then
check `Status()` in the deferred cleanup; only write the 504 header if
no response has been started (i.e., `Status() == 0`).

thrash_preventing_defs.name: Status
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms Status() returns 0 when no header has been written, which is the correct sentinel to check

thrash_preventing_defs.name: NewWrapResponseWriter
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms the constructor signature and that it takes protoMajor to select the right writer implementation

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is the edited Timeout function. An AI agent would also read NewWrapResponseWriter and Status() in wrap_writer.go to confirm the constructor signature and the meaning of Status() == 0 before writing the fix — without these reads the agent might use the wrong sentinel value or wrong constructor arguments.
